warnings: Absolute pKa is highly sensitive to the G(H+,aq) reference (~1.4 unit shift between Tissandier 1998 and Kelly 2006). Prefer mode='reference' against a known acid in the same family.

Single-conformer result: every species was evaluated at ONE geometry, with no conformational/Boltzmann averaging. For a flexible molecule the Boltzmann-averaged free energy over conformers can differ from a single conformer by several kcal/mol — shifting pKa by >1 unit, E° by >0.1 V, and logP by >0.5. If the molecule has rotatable bonds, run conformer-search first and average, or treat this as a screening estimate tied to the input geometry.

prose:
Method provenance
Property: Aqueous acidity constant pKa (absolute mode), unitless.
Level of theory: PM7 (semi-empirical NDDO) as implemented in MOPAC2016.
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian structures for HA (methanol, charge 0) and A⁻ (methoxide, charge −1). Each species was pre-optimized and frequency-analyzed at PM7; no further re-optimization or conformer search was requested (single-conformer evaluation).
Solvent/environment: COSMO implicit water in MOPAC (continuum model, no explicit solvent) — “absolute” mode uses the Tissandier 1998 free energy of the aqueous proton.
Charge & multiplicity: HA 0/1, A⁻ −1/1 (closed-shell singlets throughout).
Software: MOPAC2016 (via chemkit pka-acidity skill).
Convergence & caveats: Both species optimized without imaginary frequencies (minima). SCF and geometry optimizations converged. See live engine log (path was printed at job launch) and JSON for full details. Note density-fit not applicable to PM7.
Warnings (verbatim from engine):
• Absolute pKa is highly sensitive to the G(H+,aq) reference (~1.4 unit shift between Tissandier 1998 and Kelly 2006). Prefer mode='reference' against a known acid in the same family.
• Single-conformer result: every species was evaluated at ONE geometry, with no conformational/Boltzmann averaging. For a flexible molecule the Boltzmann-averaged free energy over conformers can differ from a single conformer by several kcal/mol — shifting pKa by >1 unit, E° by >0.1 V, and logP by >0.5. If the molecule has rotatable bonds, run conformer-search first and average, or treat this as a screening estimate tied to the input geometry.

Headline result
The computed absolute aqueous pKa of methanol (CH3OH ⇌ CH3O⁻ + H⁺) at 298.15 K is:

pKa = –247.2099982522137

This extremely negative value reflects known limitations of semi-empirical PM7 absolute pKa estimates: they are suitable only for qualitative, high-throughput screening, not quantitative prediction. No conformer averaging or higher-level corrections were applied.

Reproducibility
Command: /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py pka --method mopac --solvent water --mode absolute --ha /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/pka-acidity-validation/methanol/ha.xyz --a-minus /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/pka-acidity-validation/methanol/a_minus.xyz --out <result>.json
The calculation streamed live to a file pka_<timestamp>.out (path was printed at launch) containing full SCF and optimization details. The parsed result JSON is available at <result>.json.
